It belongs to the genus Chaenorhinum, which is a group of small, annual herbs native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Chaenorhinum montserratiorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
